signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 114.231 — AUD outperformance (avg +0.695%) vs JPY weakness (avg -0.515%) creates a 1.210% G8 spread, the widest in the complex. RBA at 4.35% (HIKING) vs BoJ 0.75% (ON HOLD) provides a +360bp carry premium, fueling the divergence. JPY COT LF net short -61,340 (crowded carry) vs AUD COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) confirms institutional alignment. Retail 88% short AUD/JPY diverges from COT, suggesting a squeeze if 115.00 is tested. Carry dominates while VIX remains at 17.9 (normal volatility).

signals.title: USD/JPY — Headline + Carry + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.875 — Hawkish Fed repricing following hotter US CPI supports DXY (98.53) while JPY extends declines. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% (+300bp) maintains the structural bid. USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation has broken to +0.529 (z=-1.93σ vs norm +0.089), indicating JPY is now hyper-sensitive to US rates compared to historical norms. Intervention risk remains the primary ceiling as price tests the 158.00 handle. Watch for US PPI data for further USD momentum.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5928 — COT LF net short -19,294 (long% at extreme 18.5%) shows specs heavily fading the NZD despite the RBNZ market bias for a Hike. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% (+150bp USD carry) supports the current level, but the crowded spec short creates significant squeeze risk if the RBNZ delivers a hawkish surprise at the 27 May meeting. Invalidation of the bearish spec thesis occurs on a sustained break above 0.6000.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Headline + Positioning
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1701 — Euro softens following French headline inflation surge (HICP +2.5%) and ahead of Eurozone GDP. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (+175bp) favors USD carry. EUR COT LF net short -21,011 (long% 44.7%) reflects neutral-to-bearish institutional sentiment. EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.697 (z=+1.54σ) suggests EUR is currently trading as a high-beta proxy for European equities rather than on rate differentials alone. Support holds at 1.1680.
